words.definitionEn: in the space or period of time separating two or more points, objects, etc. or two dates, events, etc.
words.exampleEn: The house was near a park but there was a road in between.

words.definitionEn: used when people or animals performing an action are also affected by it
words.exampleEn: They seemed to be enjoying themselves.

words.definitionEn: having a larger distance between opposite sides or surfaces than other similar objects or than normal
words.exampleEn: a thick slice of bread

words.definitionEn: a person who steals something from another person or place
words.exampleEn: a car/jewel thief

words.definitionEn: having a smaller distance between opposite sides or surfaces than other similar objects or than normal
words.exampleEn: Cut the vegetables into thin strips.

words.definitionEn: the process of thinking about something
words.exampleEn: I had to do some quick thinking.

words.definitionEn: each of three equal parts of something
words.exampleEn: He divided the money into thirds.

words.definitionEn: something that you think of or remember
words.exampleEn: thought of doing something, I couldn't bear the thought of waiting any longer.

words.definitionEn: to send something from your hand through the air by moving your hand or arm quickly
words.exampleEn: Practise throwing and catching.

words.definitionEn: arranged neatly and with everything in order
words.exampleEn: a tidy desk

words.definitionEn: to make something look neat by putting things in the place where they belong
words.exampleEn: I spent all morning cleaning and tidying.

words.definitionEn: a long, narrow piece of cloth worn around the neck, especially by men, with a knot in front
words.exampleEn: He was dressed impeccably in a suit and tie.

words.definitionEn: to attach or hold two or more things together using string, rope, etc.; to fasten somebody/something with string, rope, etc.
words.exampleEn: tie something + adv./prep., She tied the newspapers in a bundle.

words.definitionEn: a small piece of advice about something practical
words.exampleEn: tip on/for doing something, handy tips for buying a computer

words.definitionEn: an instrument such as a hammer, screwdriver, saw, etc. that you hold in your hand and use for making things, repairing things, etc.
words.exampleEn: garden tools

words.definitionEn: highest in position
words.exampleEn: He lives on the top floor.

words.definitionEn: the highest part or point of something
words.exampleEn: at the top of something, She was standing at the top of the stairs.

words.definitionEn: 1 000 000 000; one thousand million
words.exampleEn: Worldwide sales reached 2.5 billion.

words.definitionEn: to put your hand or another part of your body onto somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Don't touch that plate—it's hot!

words.definitionEn: a journey made for pleasure during which several different towns, countries, etc. are visited
words.exampleEn: a walking/sightseeing/bus tour

words.definitionEn: the business activity connected with providing accommodation, services and entertainment for people who are visiting a place for pleasure
words.exampleEn: The area is heavily dependent on tourism.

words.definitionEn: a container that you put waste in
words.exampleEn: a rubbish bin

words.definitionEn: in the direction of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: They were heading towards the German border.

words.definitionEn: a piece of cloth or paper used for drying things, especially your body
words.exampleEn: Help yourself to a clean towel.

words.definitionEn: a tall narrow building or part of a building, especially of a church or castle
words.exampleEn: a bell tower

words.definitionEn: made as a copy of a particular thing and used for playing with
words.exampleEn: a toy car

words.definitionEn: an object for children to play with
words.exampleEn: cuddly/stuffed toys

words.definitionEn: rails (= metal bars) that a train moves along
words.exampleEn: railway/railroad tracks

words.definitionEn: a belief, custom or way of doing something that has existed for a long time among a particular group of people; a set of these beliefs or customs
words.exampleEn: religious/cultural/ancient traditions

words.definitionEn: being part of the beliefs, customs or way of life of a particular group of people, that have not changed for a long time
words.exampleEn: traditional dress/music/art/culture/dance

words.definitionEn: to teach a person or an animal the skills for a particular job or activity; to be taught in this way
words.exampleEn: train somebody/something, highly trained professionals

words.definitionEn: a shoe that you wear for sports or as informal clothing
words.exampleEn: a pair of trainers

words.definitionEn: the process of learning the skills that you need to do a job
words.exampleEn: staff training

words.definitionEn: the scientific study of the life and structure of plants and animals
words.exampleEn: a degree in biology

words.definitionEn: a system for carrying people or goods from one place to another using vehicles, roads, etc.
words.exampleEn: air/freight/road transport

words.definitionEn: to succeed in reaching a particular goal, status or standard, especially by making an effort for a long time
words.exampleEn: He had finally achieved success.

words.definitionEn: a person who is travelling or who often travels
words.exampleEn: She is a frequent traveller to Belgium.

words.definitionEn: the time when a baby is born; the process of being born
words.exampleEn: at birth, The baby weighed three kilos at birth.

words.definitionEn: a problem, worry, difficulty, etc. or a situation causing this
words.exampleEn: We have trouble getting staff.

words.definitionEn: a small flat dry cake for one person, usually sweet, and baked until hard
words.exampleEn: a packet/tin of chocolate biscuits

words.definitionEn: a large vehicle for carrying heavy loads by road
words.exampleEn: a truck driver

words.definitionEn: rather; to some extent
words.exampleEn: These trousers are a bit tight.

words.definitionEn: used to describe one of a pair of children who are twins
words.exampleEn: a twin brother/sister

words.definitionEn: one of two children born at the same time to the same mother
words.exampleEn: She's expecting twins.

words.definitionEn: having the usual qualities or features of a particular type of person, thing or group
words.exampleEn: a typical Italian cafe

words.definitionEn: under the surface of the ground
words.exampleEn: an underground bunker/tunnel

words.definitionEn: under the surface of the ground
words.exampleEn: Rescuers found victims trapped several feet underground.

words.definitionEn: the knowledge that somebody has about a particular subject or situation
words.exampleEn: Students will gain a broad understanding of the workings of Parliament.

words.definitionEn: used to say that a particular situation or fact makes you sad or disappointed, or gets you into a difficult position
words.exampleEn: Unfortunately, I won't be able to attend the meeting.

words.definitionEn: not happy; sad
words.exampleEn: to feel/look/seem/sound/become unhappy

words.definitionEn: the special set of clothes worn by all members of an organization or a group at work, or by children at school
words.exampleEn: The hat is part of the school uniform.

words.definitionEn: a single thing, person or group that is complete by itself but can also form part of something larger
words.exampleEn: After 1946 the British Government treated the four territorial divisions as a single unit.

words.definitionEn: joined together as a political unit or by shared aims
words.exampleEn: the United States of America

words.definitionEn: different from what is usual or normal
words.exampleEn: The case is highly unusual.

words.definitionEn: on a floor of a house or other building that is higher than the one that you are on
words.exampleEn: an upstairs room/window/bathroom

words.definitionEn: the act of using something; the state of being used
words.exampleEn: A ban was imposed on the use of chemical weapons.

words.definitionEn: used to say that something happened continuously or frequently during a period in the past
words.exampleEn: I used to live in London.

words.definitionEn: a person or thing that uses something
words.exampleEn: road users

words.definitionEn: that happens or is done most of the time or in most cases
words.exampleEn: This is the usual way of doing it.

words.definitionEn: empty, with nothing written, printed or recorded on it
words.exampleEn: Sign your name in the blank space below.

words.definitionEn: an area of low land between hills or mountains, often with a river flowing through it; the land that a river flows through
words.exampleEn: in a valley, In the valley below cows were grazing peacefully.

words.definitionEn: a covered vehicle with no side windows in its back half, usually smaller than a lorry, used for carrying goods or people
words.exampleEn: the driver of a white van

words.definitionEn: an empty space on a printed form or document for you to write answers, information, etc. in
words.exampleEn: Please fill in the blanks.

words.definitionEn: several different sorts of the same thing
words.exampleEn: There is a wide variety of patterns to choose from.

words.definitionEn: a thing that is used for transporting people or goods from one place to another, such as a car or lorry
words.exampleEn: motor vehicles (= cars, buses, lorries, etc.)

words.definitionEn: what you can see from a particular place or position, especially beautiful countryside
words.exampleEn: view of something, There were magnificent views of the surrounding countryside.

words.definitionEn: a living thing, too small to be seen without a microscope, that causes disease in people, animals and plants
words.exampleEn: the flu/influenza virus

words.definitionEn: the sound or sounds produced through the mouth by a person speaking or singing
words.exampleEn: I could hear voices in the next room.

words.definitionEn: an act of waiting; an amount of time waited
words.exampleEn: The wait seemed interminable.

words.definitionEn: a situation in which two or more countries or groups of people fight against each other over a period of time
words.exampleEn: the Second World War

words.definitionEn: an act of cleaning somebody/something using water and usually soap
words.exampleEn: These towels are ready for a wash.

words.definitionEn: the act of cleaning something using water and usually soap
words.exampleEn: a gentle shampoo for frequent washing

words.definitionEn: a raised line of water that moves across the surface of the sea, ocean, etc.
words.exampleEn: Huge waves were breaking on the shore.

words.definitionEn: not physically strong
words.exampleEn: She is still weak after her illness.

words.definitionEn: a system for finding information on the internet, in which documents are connected to other documents
words.exampleEn: to surf/browse/search the web

words.definitionEn: a marriage ceremony, and the meal or party that usually follows it
words.exampleEn: I dreamed of having a big wedding, with all my family and friends.

words.definitionEn: how heavy somebody/something is, which can be measured in, for example, kilograms or pounds
words.exampleEn: in weight, It is about 76 kilos in weight.

words.definitionEn: something that you do or say to somebody when they arrive, especially something that makes them feel you are happy to see them
words.exampleEn: to get/receive a welcome

words.definitionEn: covered with or containing liquid, especially water
words.exampleEn: wet clothes/hair/grass

words.definitionEn: the red liquid that flows through the bodies of humans and animals
words.exampleEn: He lost a lot of blood in the accident.

words.definitionEn: one of the round objects under a car, bicycle, bus, etc. that turns when it moves
words.exampleEn: He braked suddenly, causing the front wheels to skid.

words.definitionEn: during the time that something is happening; at the same time as something else is happening
words.exampleEn: We must have been burgled while we were asleep.

words.definitionEn: to send out air from the mouth
words.exampleEn: + adv./prep., You're not blowing hard enough!

words.definitionEn: full; complete
words.exampleEn: Let's forget the whole thing.

words.definitionEn: used in questions to ask who something belongs to
words.exampleEn: Whose house is that?

words.definitionEn: used in questions to ask who something belongs to
words.exampleEn: Whose house is that?

words.definitionEn: measuring a large distance from one side to the other
words.exampleEn: It's a wide, fast-flowing river.

words.definitionEn: living or growing in natural conditions; not kept in a house or on a farm
words.exampleEn: wild animals/birds/flowers

words.definitionEn: a long thin piece of strong hard material, especially wood, used, for example, for making floors, building walls and roofs and making boats
words.exampleEn: He had ripped up the carpet, leaving only the bare boards.

words.definitionEn: a person, a team, an animal, etc. that wins something
words.exampleEn: The lucky winner gets an all-expenses-paid trip to Sydney.

words.definitionEn: used especially in a letter, email or card to say that you hope that somebody will be happy, well or successful
words.exampleEn: Give my good wishes to the family.

words.definitionEn: to want something to happen or to be true even though it is unlikely or impossible
words.exampleEn: wish (that)…, I wish I were taller.

words.definitionEn: the hard material that the trunk and branches of a tree are made of; this material when it is used to build or make things with, or as a fuel
words.exampleEn: He chopped some wood for the fire.

words.definitionEn: made of wood
words.exampleEn: a wooden box/door/floor

words.definitionEn: having a job for which you are paid
words.exampleEn: the working population

words.definitionEn: thinking about unpleasant things that have happened or that might happen and therefore feeling unhappy and afraid
words.exampleEn: Don't look so worried!

words.definitionEn: to keep thinking about unpleasant things that might happen or about problems that you have
words.exampleEn: Don't worry. We have plenty of time.

words.definitionEn: of poorer quality or lower standard; more unpleasant
words.exampleEn: The rooms were awful and the food was worse.

words.definitionEn: when a liquid boils or when you boil it, it is heated to the point where it forms bubbles and turns to steam or vapour
words.exampleEn: The water was bubbling and boiling away.

words.definitionEn: of the poorest quality or lowest standard; worse than any other person or thing of a similar kind
words.exampleEn: It was by far the worst speech he had ever made.

words.definitionEn: used to show that you are very surprised or impressed by somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Wow! You look terrific!

words.definitionEn: used in negative sentences and questions to talk about something that has not happened but that you expect to happen
words.exampleEn: (British English), I haven't received a letter from him yet.

words.definitionEn: of or belonging to you
words.exampleEn: Is that book yours?
Five, four, three, two, one, zero… We have lift-off.

words.definitionEn: any of the hard parts that form the skeleton of the body of a human or an animal
words.exampleEn: He survived the accident with no broken bones.

words.definitionEn: to arrange to have or use something on a particular date in the future; to buy a ticket in advance
words.exampleEn: Book early to avoid disappointment.

words.definitionEn: to take and use something that belongs to somebody else, and return it to them at a later time
words.exampleEn: borrow something, Can I borrow your umbrella?

words.definitionEn: a person who is in charge of other people at work and tells them what to do
words.exampleEn: I'll ask my boss if I can have the day off.

words.definitionEn: in the lowest, last or furthest place or position
words.exampleEn: your bottom lip

words.definitionEn: the lowest part of something
words.exampleEn: the bottom of the screen/stairs/hill

words.definitionEn: a deep round dish with a wide open top, used especially for holding food or liquid
words.exampleEn: a salad/fruit/sugar bowl

words.definitionEn: the organ inside the head that controls movement, thought, memory and feeling
words.exampleEn: The human brain is a complex organ.

words.definitionEn: a structure that is built over a road, railway, river, etc. so that people, vehicles, etc. can cross from one side to the other
words.exampleEn: We crossed the bridge over the River Windrush.

words.definitionEn: full of light; shining strongly
words.exampleEn: bright light/sunshine

words.definitionEn: extremely clever or impressive
words.exampleEn: What a brilliant idea!

words.definitionEn: to do something for a particular purpose or in order to deal with a situation
words.exampleEn: act to do something, It is vital that we act to stop the destruction of the rainforests.

words.definitionEn: that has been damaged or injured; no longer whole or working correctly
words.exampleEn: a broken window/plate

words.definitionEn: an object made of short stiff hairs (called bristles) or wires set in a block of wood or plastic, usually attached to a handle. Brushes are used for many different jobs, such as cleaning, painting and tidying your hair.
words.exampleEn: a paintbrush

words.definitionEn: to clean, polish or make something smooth with a brush
words.exampleEn: brush something, to brush your hair/teeth

words.definitionEn: to produce flames and heat
words.exampleEn: A welcoming fire was burning in the fireplace.

words.definitionEn: a man who works in business, especially at a high level
words.exampleEn: a group of visiting Italian politicians and businessmen

words.definitionEn: a small round piece of metal, plastic, etc. that is sewn onto a piece of clothing and used for fastening two parts together
words.exampleEn: (British English), to do up/undo your buttons

words.definitionEn: always busy doing things, especially physical activities
words.exampleEn: Staying physically active in later years can also keep you feeling younger.

words.definitionEn: a place where people live temporarily in tents or temporary buildings
words.exampleEn: Let's return to camp.

words.definitionEn: to put up a tent and live in it for a short time
words.exampleEn: I camped overnight in a field.

words.definitionEn: living in a tent, etc. on holiday
words.exampleEn: Do you go camping?

words.definitionEn: a metal container in which food and drink is sold
words.exampleEn: can of something, a can of beans/beer/soda

words.definitionEn: the process of caring for somebody/something and providing what they need for their health or protection
words.exampleEn: Some people were badly injured and needed medical care.

words.definitionEn: to feel that something is important and worth worrying about
words.exampleEn: I don't care (= I will not be upset) if I never see him again!

words.definitionEn: giving attention or thought to what you are doing so that you avoid hurting yourself, damaging something or doing something wrong
words.exampleEn: Be careful!

words.definitionEn: with a lot of attention or thought, so that you avoid hurting yourself, damaging something or doing something wrong
words.exampleEn: Drive carefully.

words.definitionEn: a piece of thick woven material made of wool, etc., used to cover the floor of a room or stairs; the material used for carpets
words.exampleEn: He spent the morning laying the new carpet.

words.definitionEn: a humorous drawing in a newspaper or magazine, especially one about politics or events in the news
words.exampleEn: a political/satirical cartoon

words.definitionEn: a particular situation or a situation of a particular type
words.exampleEn: in… cases, In some cases people have had to wait several weeks for an appointment.
